resources.string:
This app needs root permissions to work.
Please grant root access (Magisk prompt) and tap Retry.
Alerts about blocked attempts to scan the system
The app “%1$s” is analyzing the system. Access was blocked.
There is a suspicion that “%1$s” attempted to scan the system. Access was blocked.

A module update is staged (versionCode=%1$d).
Reboot the device so Magisk can apply the new version.

A module install/update is staged.
Reboot the device so Magisk can apply the changes.

Installed module version: versionCode=%1$d. Bundled with app: versionCode=%2$d.
Updating is recommended for best compatibility. You may skip, but some features may be unavailable and API mismatch errors are possible.
The installed module version is no longer supported (versionCode=%1$d). To continue using ZDT-D, update the module.
Backup not created: no folders were added to the archive.
Backup not created: no folders were added to the archive. %1$s
Backup created, but tar reported a warning: %1$s
You selected a version below %1$s. Startup issues are possible, and built-in strategies may not work.

Incomplete module installation/update detected.
Reboot is required for Magisk to apply changes.
Legacy or non-standard module structure detected (system folder).
It may be a fake or incompatible version. Reinstall the module for safe and correct operation.
To keep using ZDT-D, you need to update the module.
Installer not detected. Automatic installation is not possible.
We can save the module archive to /sdcard, then you can install it manually in your root manager.
We can save the module archive to /sdcard for manual installation.
If an old version is installed, it will be removed before installation.
No data to back up: no folders found in working_folder.
Root is required to import a backup
This is not a ZDT-D backup
Couldn’t send stop command. Try again later.
Couldn’t wait for the service to stop. Try again later.
No folders found in the backup.
Settings restored, but the service didn’t start.
No staged module update found. Install the module update first and wait for the reboot prompt.
No previous version settings found (working_folder folder is missing).
No data to migrate: no folders found in working_folder.
No staged module update found (modules_update/ZDT-D is missing).
Checks are performed in the background when you open the app (no more than once every 12 hours).
Show notifications about service status and security alerts (running / stopped, blocked system scan attempts).
Auto: Russian if your system language is Russian, otherwise English.
To update the app online, you need to allow installing updates from this app. This is only required to install the APK after download.
Service is active. Settings changes apply after stop/start.
Service is stopped. Tap the button to start.
ZDT-D is a root module (Magisk / KernelSU / APatch) designed to bypass DPI (deep packet inspection) on the internet. It helps bypass service blocks and throttling.
• Bypass blocks and speed limits\n• Convenient graphical interface for configuration
• Privacy & security: the module does not collect personal data and does not harm device functionality.\n• Freedom & openness: completely free and will remain so.
Unsupported architecture. ZDT-D works only on arm64-v8a. Current device: %1$s.

ZDT-D is a root module (Magisk / KernelSU / APatch) to bypass DPI blocks and traffic restrictions. Root is required because the module runs system scripts and networking components at OS level — something regular apps can’t do.\n\nRoot is also used to install/update the module, control the daemon, and read module files (token and logs). Root requests are always confirmed via your root manager, and permissions can be revoked at any time.
Waiting for the root manager response…
Root not granted. If you tapped “Deny”, try again.
To complete module installation/update, you need to reboot the device.
The old module version will be removed before installation.
Tap “Install” to install the ZDT-D module from the app package. If an installer is detected (Magisk / KernelSU / APatch), installation will be automatic.
Module installation requires Android 9 or newer. Current Android version: %1$s.
Android 9/10 are not officially supported. You will be asked to confirm before installing.

Official support starts with Android 11+. Android 9 and Android 10 have not been tested. Installation on these versions is at your own risk. If any problem occurs, responsibility is entirely on the user. Continue?
A reboot is required to apply changes.
Open your root manager (Magisk / KernelSU / APatch) and install this ZIP as a module.
Warning: conflicting modules were detected. Please remove these modules, as incorrect operation of ZDT-D is possible.
A DNSCrypt module was detected. It is recommended to remove this module. If you need DNSCrypt, it is already included in ZDT-D.
This module has a high level of conflict. The functionality of this module is already available in ZDT-D.

The Zygisk component is bundled with the ZDT-D installer and is recommended if you want additional protection against analyzer apps.
It hides VPN interfaces created by the module, making the environment cleaner for unwanted apps.

Installing and running the Zygisk component requires Zygisk to be enabled in your root manager: Magisk, KernelSU, APatch, or a compatible solution.
If Zygisk is not enabled, the component will not load and your root manager may disable the module.
Install the Zygisk component?

KernelSU/APatch do not provide built-in Zygisk like regular Magisk. This component requires a compatible Zygisk layer to be installed, enabled, and running, for example ZygiskNext, ZygiskOnKernelSU, or a similar solution.
Minimum versions: KernelSU: 10940+ APatch: 10700+
The install log says the Zygisk component could not be installed. You can repeat ZDT-D installation without the Zygisk component.
Archive structure changed. Automatic update is not possible.
No previous version settings found (modules/ZDT-D/working_folder is missing).
Next, install this archive in your root manager (Magisk / KernelSU / APatch) as a regular module.
Manifest file not found in the archive. This is not a ZDT-D backup.
Manifest file is empty or can’t be read.
Select installed apps; package names are saved automatically.
Name: English only, no spaces (spaces become _), max 10 chars.
Select Opera server region used by opera-proxy (-country).
Links to the project, releases, community and author support.
Allows the tool to start without a selected app list
Tap to add. (No checkmarks here — remove from the top list.)
The daemon is online, but it returned an empty tool list.
The daemon is offline. Start ZDT-D first.
Open a tool to edit configs, profiles and app lists.
Opera Proxy chain with ByeDPI and t2s
Save all folders from working_folder into a single archive, then restore them if needed. The flag.sha256 file is not included.
Current settings will be completely replaced with the contents of the archive. The service will be stopped during the operation.
The file will be deleted and cannot be recovered.
Updates Zapret / Zapret 2 binaries and Lua files directly from GitHub releases. Check is performed only when you tap the button.
Stop the service before checking/updating. This is required so rules and processes don’t use the files during replacement.
You selected %1$s, which is below %2$s. Startup issues are possible, and built-in strategies may not work.
Choose a version to install. Latest (auto) is recommended.
Use the most recent GitHub release
Files from dnscrypt/setting (edit existing files only).
No additional files found in dnscrypt/setting.
Editing is disabled for this file because it exceeds the size limit (%1$d bytes).
File: %1$s Size: %2$s bytes Use another editor to modify it.
Try Refresh or reopen this file.
Can’t load file (maybe too large or server error).
Use a safe name (letters, digits, dot, dash, underscore).

Editing is disabled: file is larger than %1$d bytes.
Use upload/replace or edit externally.

Path:
/data/adb/modules/ZDT-D/strategic/%2$s/%3$s

Editing is disabled: file is larger than %1$d bytes.
Use upload/replace or edit externally.

Path:
/data/adb/modules/ZDT-D/strategic/%2$s/%3$s

Size: %4$d bytes
Profile config. You can use a prebuilt strategy or keep your own.
Tap a config to apply it immediately.
Service is still running. Stop it and try again.
Select the backup again (or reopen this window) before restoring again.
Remove the module and uninstall the app from this device.
Do you confirm deletion of the module and the app?
After uninstalling the app, the device will reboot automatically to complete the module removal.
Thank you for using this app. You can always come back — we will be waiting for you!
Please uninstall the app manually. After the app is removed, cleanup of the app and module will continue, then the device will reboot automatically.
Preparing for removal: stopping services and checking status...

Backup is not supported because the versionCode differs (backup: %1$s, module: %2$s). Due to differences in file locations between versions, restore is available only when versionCode matches. A clean setup is recommended to avoid version mismatch issues.
This backup was created for a different module version. You can try restoring it anyway, but it may break the service due to differences in the module architecture.

Possible issues: the daemon may fail to start, tools may not work, or settings may be corrupted. If anything goes wrong, the only fix may be a clean module install.
By continuing, you agree to restore the backup while ignoring the versionCode check.
Backup is not supported because it was created by an old module architecture (versionCode: %1$s). Minimum supported backup versionCode is %2$s.
Restore is not supported because the currently installed module/app architecture is too old (versionCode: %1$s). Minimum supported versionCode is %2$s.
Preparing the interface and checking the connection to the module.
Failed to connect to the daemon. Module found, but it may be working incorrectly. It is recommended to reinstall the module.
Failed to connect to the daemon. Module found, but its structure looks incorrect. It is recommended to reinstall the module.
Failed to connect to the daemon. The module directory was not found. Install the module again.
The service was paused or stopped. Controls are temporarily unavailable. The app is automatically checking whether the connection is restored.
The Enabled switch is used only in socks5 mode.
This function is currently being reworked and is temporarily unavailable.
Failed to update listen_port in config.json
Config saved, but the profile port update failed
The active transparent profile was not found
Manage opera-proxy servers. Each server stores SNI, optional ByeDPI, and an optional server address.
No servers configured yet. Create one to get started.
Controls how the module handles sleep and memory restrictions.
Higher battery use, but maximum module stability.
Protection is disabled. If you notice issues, enable it.
Works only if the system reports correct screen state to the module.
Manage mode, ports, imported links, and server cards from one place.
Each server has its own card, config, and port settings.
Name: English characters only, no spaces (spaces become _), maximum 10 characters.
Create a new sing-box server automatically from a one-line link without editing JSON manually.
Beta feature: parsing may fail for some links or uncommon parameters.
Beta feature. Review the result carefully if you use uncommon parameters.
Paste a one-line config such as vless://, vmess://, trojan://, ss:// or socks://. A new server and its settings will be created automatically.
A free mixed-in port will be assigned automatically. Suggested port: %1$d.
Supported: vless, vmess, trojan, shadowsocks, socks. Full JSON is also accepted.
listen_port %1$d was found in config.json. Save this port to server %2$s?
Port %1$d is already used by server %2$s. Suggested free port: %3$d. Replace the port inside config.json and save it to server %4$s?
Allows routing hotspot traffic through the selected tool.
Enabling this feature may reduce device security because the tool port will be exposed. Anyone on the network may try to connect to your proxy.
Choose a sing-box profile below. The hotspot profile choice is reset every time hotspot is enabled, so it must be selected again.
Hold the sing-box button to choose a profile for hotspot.
No sing-box profile selected yet. If you close settings now, hotspot will be turned off automatically.
There are no enabled sing-box profiles available.
Blocks QUIC traffic for selected apps.
This feature blocks QUIC traffic for the app. It forces the app to use TCP as the primary connection.
Selected apps will be forced to use TCP instead of QUIC whenever possible.
Failed to save QUIC blocking settings
Apps are already in port scan protection
These apps are already added to port scan protection. Remove them from the QUIC blocking list selection.
Blocks apps from detecting the open ports of the module.

This feature blocks apps from detecting the open ports of the module, so the app cannot determine whether ZDT-D is installed on your device.
Protection does not work in hotspot mode. If hotspot is enabled and sing-box is selected, protection will not apply to it.
Selected apps will not be able to detect the open ports of the ZDT-D module.
Rebuild the module app list if an app was removed and installed again.
This reset forces the module to rebuild the app list. Use it if you removed an app and installed it again — the app identifier may have changed and needs to be refreshed.
The procedure will run automatically, the module will fetch the required data itself.
These apps are already used in tool rules. Remove them from those tools and add them to port scan protection?
Each sing-box profile has its own t2s ports, apps list, and server set.
No servers created for this profile yet.
t2s_port and t2s_web_port must be different
Changes are saved automatically after a short pause.
Port and enabled state are saved automatically.
Set the required SNI and, optionally, a server address in host:port format.
Use a valid host:port value, for example 77.111.244.11:443
WireProxy profiles with local SOCKS5 bridge
Hotspot traffic will be redirected through the selected WireProxy profile.
Select a WireProxy profile to continue.
Select the apps whose traffic should use this WireProxy profile.
Configure the local t2s ports for this WireProxy profile. Changes are saved automatically.
Changes are saved automatically after a short pause.
The t2s port and t2s web port must be different.
Resolve the port conflicts before saving.
Create, edit and delete servers inside this WireProxy profile. SOCKS5 uses 127.0.0.1 only.
SOCKS5 endpoint will be generated automatically on save.
You can leave the name blank and let the daemon generate it automatically.
Optional. Allowed: latin letters, digits, _ and -
A server with this name already exists.
Edit the wireproxy config. If [Socks5] BindAddress is missing, the app will generate a local 127.0.0.1 port automatically.
[Socks5] BindAddress is missing and could not be generated.
A local SOCKS5 BindAddress will be generated automatically.
Tor with local bridge routing and custom bridge list
Enable or disable Tor for the selected apps.
Before enabling Tor, add at least one bridge, set UseBridges 1 and make sure SocksPort uses 127.0.0.1.
Select the apps whose traffic should use Tor.
Choose your Tor SOCKS5 port and local t2s ports. Missing values are generated automatically, and valid changes are saved automatically.
Valid changes are saved automatically after a short pause.
Configure the local t2s ports for Tor.
Enter both t2s ports in the range 1..65535.
The SOCKS5, t2s and t2s web ports must all be different.
This field changes SocksPort inside torrc. Only 127.0.0.1:PORT is allowed.
Enter a valid port in the range 1..65535.
The port is saved into torrc as SocksPort 127.0.0.1:PORT.
Create, edit and delete Tor bridges. When creating a bridge, you can paste one or several lines. Supported bridge protocols: obfs4, webtunnel, snowflake and meek_lite.
Request bridge parameters from @GetBridgesBot. You can paste one or several bridge lines returned by the bot.
Paste one or several bridge lines. Supported protocols: obfs4, webtunnel, snowflake and meek_lite.
Failed to open the bot link.
Enter at least one valid bridge line before saving.
Paste one or several bridge lines. Raw obfs4, webtunnel, snowflake and meek_lite lines are supported.
Only obfs4, webtunnel, snowflake and meek_lite bridge lines are supported.
Editing supports only one bridge line at a time.
Full torrc editor. Raw bridge lines from Telegram can be pasted here too.
Add at least one bridge before enabling Tor.
Profile-based SOCKS5 upstream bridge through t2s
Select the apps whose traffic should use this myproxy profile.
Configure local t2s ports for this profile. Valid changes are saved automatically.
The t2s port and t2s web port must be different.
Valid changes are saved automatically after a short pause.
Each profile uses one SOCKS5 upstream server.
If username or password is used, fill in both fields.
SOCKS5/HTTP(S) proxy used by opera-proxy to reach Opera backend API. You may specify one proxy URL, several proxy URLs separated by commas, or a URL to a plain text proxy list.
Use proxy URLs with a scheme: http://, https://, socks5:// or socks5h://. You can also use an http(s) URL to a plain text proxy list.

Format: http://, https://, socks5:// or socks5h://[login:password@]host[:port]. Multiple proxies can be separated by commas. You may also paste an http(s) URL to a plain text list where each line contains a proxy URL. The list is downloaded again on every Opera-proxy start. Addresses without a scheme are not supported. If no working proxy is found or the list cannot be downloaded, Opera-proxy starts without api-proxy.
Download limit in bytes for server speed test. Default: 204800 (200 KB)
Retry interval on startup failure. Examples: 3s, 5s, 10s
Log verbosity level. Higher = more logs. Default: 50
User-Agent sent to Opera API. Change only if you know what you are doing.
DNS resolvers used by opera-proxy to resolve Opera server addresses. The local DNSCrypt resolver (if enabled) is always prepended automatically.
Resolver list must not be empty
Profile-based launcher with optional app mode and t2s
Valid changes are saved automatically after a short pause.
Enable app-based mode to use the app list together with t2s.
If the parsed app list becomes empty, the profile will not start.
With app mode disabled, the profile works as a launcher for the custom command without the app list or t2s.
Select the apps whose traffic should use this myprogram profile.
Configure the local t2s ports and SOCKS5 credentials for the t2s mode.
The t2s port and t2s web port must be different.
Enter the custom command used to launch this profile.
Example: ./mainbin --config ./config.json. Line breaks are flattened into one line on save.
Enter one upstream SOCKS5 port per line. Used only in t2s mode.
Enter one protected port per line.
Each non-empty line must contain a port from 1 to 65535.
Upload or remove files stored in the profile bin directory.
Apply the current profile configuration and restart it if needed.
Choose whether app traffic should use t2s or direct transparent routing.
This mode uses t2s and the upstream port list. Protocol is TCP only.
This mode does not start t2s: app traffic is redirected straight to the user program port.
Configure direct redirection of app traffic to the user program port and choose the protocol.
Choose which protocols should be redirected in transparent mode.
Local port where t2s receives app traffic.
Local service web port used by t2s.
SOCKS5 proxy address: server IP or domain.
SOCKS5 proxy port on the server.
SOCKS5 username. Leave empty if auth is not required.
SOCKS5 password. Leave empty if auth is not required.
Note: while this window is active, CPU usage may increase to render the map.
Failed to run the connection inspection command.
Root is unavailable. Grant the app superuser access.
Bind selected apps to an existing TUN interface through Android netd
myvpn binds selected apps to an already existing TUN interface.
Create a profile, add a TUN name, DNS servers, CIDR mode and an app list.
Allowed: A-Z, a-z, 0-9, underscore and hyphen. Length: 1..10 characters. No spaces, dots, slashes, Cyrillic or special symbols.
Invalid profile name. Use only A-Z, a-z, 0-9, _ or -, length 1..10.
Profile name is used as the directory name and is not edited here.
Auto-save is enabled. Changes apply after service restart.
The interface must be created by another program before myvpn is applied. Do not use wlan0, rmnet_data0, eth0, lo or dummy0.
Invalid TUN name or forbidden physical interface.
This TUN interface is already used by another VPN profile. Choose another name or create a new profile to get the next free tunN automatically.
Enter 1..8 IPv4 DNS servers separated by spaces or commas. IPv6, domains and https/tls URLs are not allowed.
The daemon will try to detect CIDR from the TUN interface.
Enter IPv4 CIDR manually if auto detection cannot be used.
Enter a valid IPv4 CIDR with prefix.
Select package names whose traffic should use this myvpn binding. UID parsing is done by the daemon.
The profile is enabled, but the app list is empty.
This app is already used by another network program. Remove it there or choose another profile.
Profile-based tun2socks routing through Android netd
tun2socks creates the TUN interface; ZDT-D routes selected apps through it.
Create a profile, add a TUN name, proxy URL and app list.
Allowed: A-Z, a-z, 0-9, underscore and hyphen. Length: 1..10 characters. No spaces, dots, slashes, Cyrillic or special symbols.
Invalid profile name. Use only A-Z, a-z, 0-9, _ or -, length 1..10.
Profile name is used as the directory name and is not edited here.
Auto-save is enabled. Changes apply after service restart.
tun2socks creates this interface. After start, the daemon assigns IPv4 and brings it up. Do not use wlan0, rmnet_data0, eth0, lo or dummy0.
Invalid TUN name or forbidden physical interface.
Proxy URL must include scheme, host and port 1..65535.
Proxy URL is empty or invalid.
Allowed: debug, info, warn, error, silent. Default: info.
Allowed values: debug, info, warn, error, silent.
Select package names whose traffic should use this tun2socks profile. UID parsing is done by the daemon.
The profile is enabled, but the app list is empty.
This app is already used by another network program. Remove it there or choose another profile.
If a profile does not start, check /data/adb/modules/ZDT-D/working_folder/tun2socks/profile/<name>/log/tun2socks.log and /data/adb/modules/ZDT-D/log/zdtd.log.
Profile-based OpenVPN routing through Android netd
OpenVPN profile routing through Android netd for selected apps.
Create a profile, add client.ovpn, DNS servers and an app list.
Allowed: A-Z, a-z, 0-9, underscore and hyphen. Length: 1..10 characters. No spaces, dots, slashes, Cyrillic or special symbols.
Invalid profile name. Use only A-Z, a-z, 0-9, _ or -, length 1..10.
Profile name is used as the directory name and is not edited here.
client.ovpn is missing. The profile cannot start without it.
The interface must appear after OpenVPN starts. Do not use physical interfaces like wlan0, rmnet_data0, eth0 or lo.
Invalid TUN name or forbidden physical interface.
Enter 1..8 IPv4 DNS servers separated by spaces or commas. IPv6, domains and https/tls URLs are not allowed.
Paste client.ovpn content or load it from a file. It is saved as profile/name/client.ovpn.
Select package names whose traffic should use this OpenVPN profile. UID parsing is done by the daemon.
The profile is enabled, but the app list is empty.
This app is already used by another network program. Remove it there or choose another profile.
Auto-save is enabled. Changes apply after service restart.
client.ovpn is edited separately. Use the pencil to open the editor.
Auto-save is enabled. Changes apply after service restart.
AmneziaWG userspace tunnel with netd routing
AmneziaWG uses a userspace interface; ZDT-D handles routes, DNS and app UID routing through vpn_netd.
Create a profile, add client.conf, IPv4 address, DNS servers and an app list.
Allowed: A-Z, a-z, 0-9, underscore and hyphen. Length: 1..10 characters. No spaces, dots, slashes, Cyrillic or special symbols.
Invalid profile name. Use only A-Z, a-z, 0-9, _ or -, length 1..10.
Profile name is used as the directory name and is not edited here.
Default starts from awg1. Do not use physical interfaces like wlan0, rmnet, ccmni, eth, ap, rndis or lo.
Invalid TUN name or forbidden physical interface.
Enter 1..4 IPv4 CIDR addresses separated by spaces or commas, for example 172.16.0.2/32. IPv6 is not used.
Enter 1..4 valid IPv4 CIDR addresses only.
Enter 1..8 IPv4 DNS servers separated by spaces or commas. IPv6, domains and https/tls URLs are not allowed.
Resolve the peer endpoint before applying awg setconf.
Advanced option: remove FwMark before saving client.conf for awg setconf.
Paste client.conf content or load a .conf file. Address, DNS, MTU and unsupported Interface commands are moved to profile settings by the daemon.
client.conf is missing. The profile cannot start without it.
client.conf is edited separately. Use the pencil to open the editor.
Auto-save is enabled. After saving, config and settings are reloaded because the daemon normalizes client.conf.
Address, DNS, MTU, Table, Pre/Post Up/Down and SaveConfig are moved out of client.conf into settings.
Saved and normalized. Settings were reloaded.
Select package names whose traffic should use this AmneziaWG profile. This is an exclusive network backend.
The profile is enabled, but the app list is empty. Select at least one app.
This app is already used by another network program. Remove it there or choose another profile.
Before enabling, add client.conf with PrivateKey/PublicKey, valid IPv4 address, DNS, MTU and at least one app.
Auto-save is enabled. Changes apply after service restart.
Saved. Changes apply after service restart.
Clash-compatible Mihomo YAML routed through TUN + tun2socks
Mihomo uses Clash-compatible YAML and ZDT-D routes selected apps through TUN + tun2socks.
Create a profile, edit config.yaml and select apps.
Allowed: A-Z, a-z, 0-9, underscore and hyphen. Length: 1..10 characters. No spaces, dots, slashes, Cyrillic or special symbols.
Invalid profile name. Use only A-Z, a-z, 0-9, _ or -, length 1..10.
Profile name is used as the directory name and is not edited here.
Web UI preset and local controller.
Select, fallback, test, and relay groups.
Routing rules in their exact order.
Rare and raw settings, kept compact.
external-ui is fixed to this profile path: %1$s
Auto-save is enabled for profile settings. Changes apply after service restart.
tun2socks creates this interface for Mihomo. ZDT-D assigns IPv4 and routes selected apps through it.
Invalid TUN name or forbidden physical interface.
Local 127.0.0.1 port used by tun2socks as SOCKS5 upstream. Range: 1..65535.
Port must be a number from 1 to 65535.
Port is already used by another program/profile.
Mihomo local mixed port is protected by ZDT-D. Apps are routed through TUN and should not access the local proxy port directly.
This is the user Mihomo YAML. The daemon generates config.runtime.yaml before launch and removes ZDT-D managed top-level fields.
Managed Mihomo fields found in config.yaml: %1$s. Daemon will ignore them in config.runtime.yaml, but you can remove them from user config.
Do not rely on mixed-port, allow-lan, bind-address or log-level here. ZDT-D controls them from setting.json in runtime config.
Edit the proxies section. Templates add readable YAML blocks and unknown fields are kept in raw YAML.
Edit proxy-groups as YAML. You can add common group templates and then adjust names/proxies.
Edit rules as a YAML list. Order matters.
Select package names whose traffic should use this Mihomo profile. UID parsing is done by the daemon.
The profile is enabled, but the app list is empty.
This app is already used by another network program. Remove it there or choose another profile.
Dashboard and external-controller are stored in config.yaml. Keep external-controller on 127.0.0.1 unless you intentionally expose it.
The UI does not create routes, iptables, netd rules or Mihomo tun mode. ZDT-D daemon does runtime routing.
Choose the Web UI preset and local controller values written into config.yaml. The daemon only runs the resulting YAML.
Use 127.0.0.1 by default. Do not expose 0.0.0.0 unless you intentionally open the controller.
Dashboard settings are stored in config.yaml. ZDT-D managed mixed-port still listens only on 127.0.0.1.
Card builder for proxies. Unknown fields stay inside each raw YAML block.
No proxy blocks in this section yet.
Add %1$s to the Proxy group now? If you skip this, the proxy will be saved but rules will not use it until you add it to a group.
Create and reorder proxy-groups. You can still edit each group as raw YAML.
Rules are applied from top to bottom. Move important rules higher.
Changes in this section are saved automatically after editing.
This block updates immediately while you edit the fields. Unknown Mihomo fields stay here and are preserved.
This rule is used when no previous rule matched.
This dashboard controller port is already used by another Mihomo profile.
No proxies are available yet. Create a proxy first, then add it to the group.
No proxies, groups, or built-in policies are available.
No proxy providers yet. Create a proxy provider first, then select it in use.
Raw-safe DNS editor. Unknown DNS fields are preserved.
Mihomo DNS is complex. This editor changes only the dns: section and keeps fields such as nameserver-policy, fallback-filter and proxy-server-nameserver.
Auto-save is enabled. This editor replaces only this top-level section and keeps the rest of config.yaml unchanged.
Raw-safe editors for Mihomo sections that are advanced, fast-changing or risky to simplify.
Listeners, tun, redir and tproxy are advanced Mihomo inbounds. ZDT-D runtime routing still uses netd → TUN → tun2socks → Mihomo mixed-port.
Domain sniffer settings. Do not enable automatically; edit only when you understand the rules impact.
sub-rules are preserved separately from the main rules list.
Mihomo tunnels are local TCP/UDP forwarding, not Android VPN routing.
Extra Mihomo inbound listeners. Avoid exposing local ports unless you understand the risk.
NTP settings. System time writing may require root and should be used carefully.
Experimental Mihomo options. Unknown fields are preserved.
Complex rules should be checked in raw YAML if you use AND / OR / NOT or SUB-RULE syntax.
%1$d change(s) are waiting to be saved.
The latest config change could not be written. You can retry it here instead of entering it again.
Proxy group change is not saved
Proxy %1$s is saved in proxies. Save this pending change to add it to group Proxy, or cancel to keep the config as it is.
